item.text: {"case_id":"brake-triage-p04","score":2,"pass_criteria_met":true,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":true,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"The assistant triggers the brake immediately: it says not to add the sorting-table confirmer as a standing role, identifies the repeated pattern across the prior manual gates, and reframes the issue as an upstream process/system/ownership failure. It allows only a bounded emergency confirmer for the current batch/shift while directing the real action toward consolidating the four incidents, finding the common upstream break, and implementing structural/system controls. This meets the anti-spiral criteria and avoids the fail signal of simply adding another manual checkpoint."}
